[PDF_PAGE 11 | score=2] Introduction If this avatar is destroyed. they require one full yea r to fash­ ion another. Many demigods, however. are wholly unable to employ avatars. Granted Abilities: Demigods can grant any power or spell of up to 5th level to their worshippers and priests. Heroes Heroes are legendary mortals who have performed such great feats that they become part of a culture's mythos. They have no god- like powers, although they afe genera lly well­ known to the gods and may have frequent dealings with im­ mortals. Their only powers arl!' Ihat they often have very high attribute scores, maximum hit points. and are not sub­ ject to dual- or multi-class restrictions. Divine Ascension As mentioned in t
[PDF_PAGE 67 | score=1] Arawn (Intermediate god) Morrlgan (Intermediate god) ,,;:---- 71 As god of dealh and the underworld , Arawn rarely has reason to venture into the world or the living. His home is an is­ land so far out at sea thai no one, not even Manannan mac liT. can find it while liv­ '---_ --'= _ _ J ing, for only the dead can travel there. In fact, Arawn generally only sends an avatar to the world of the living after someone has been resurrected thai Arawn would rather keep. Many other Celtic gods will endorse res­ urrections , but none can guarantee that Arawn will leave the matter at that. Arawn has absolute power over life and dealh among the Celts .
